metadata.description: NJ deal book site briefs (re-weighted for acquirability)

briefs.name: Novartis East Hanover Facility
briefs.notes: Major pharmaceutical layoffs, not full closure. Headquarters location with previous 235 layoffs in 2024. Part of company restructuring.

brief.opportunity_summary: The 230-acre Novartis campus in East Hanover is being sold off in parcels while Novartis retains ~100 acres. Russo Development & Onyx Equities acquired 62 acres for industrial; New Vernon Equities is developing an office/innovation hub; RealCold has a cold storage deal. But none of the current developers are positioning for data center use -- and the 230kV Whippany substation 0.7 miles away is the key asset they're not leveraging. Brian knows Kevin Dudley (CBRE, brokering the industrial portion) and Ed Russo personally. Ralph is on the same developer caliber. This is an insider track, not a cold call.

site_details.current_use: Closure in progress or recently completed. Site likely transitioning.
site_details.environmental_flags: Depends on prior industrial use. Phase I ESA recommended.

acquirability.owner_motivation: Novartis is actively divesting. CBRE (Kevin Dudley) and JLL (Tim Greiner) are marketing remaining parcels. Multiple deals already closed.
acquirability.estimated_acquisition_cost: $5M-$15M for a remaining 20-40 acre parcel with power positioning
acquirability.recommended_approach: 1. Call Kevin Dudley directly -- Brian knows him. Ask what parcels remain and whether a data center use has been pitched. 2. Call Ed Russo -- he already bought 62 acres. Ask if he'd JV on a DC pad or if there are adjacent parcels he passed on. 3. Position the 230kV Whippany substation proximity as the differentiator -- no other buyer is leading with power availability.
acquirability.notes: Brian's personal relationships with Dudley (CBRE) and Russo (developer) create a direct path. Ralph's developer credibility matters here -- Novartis/CBRE want serious buyers.

developer_value.estimated_developer_price: $25M-$50M for a 20-40MW data center-ready site with transmission proximity
developer_value.spread_estimate: $10M-$35M if a remaining parcel can be positioned with power
developer_value.comparable_transactions: NJ data center land with confirmed power trades at $500K-$1.5M per supportable MW. 30MW site = $15-45M developer value.

next_steps: Call Kevin Dudley (CBRE) -- ask what parcels remain and whether DC use has been proposed
Call Ed Russo -- ask about JV opportunity or adjacent parcels he didn't take
Have Brian's BPU contact confirm Whippany substation available capacity and interconnection timeline
Research East Hanover Township planning board minutes for remaining Novartis approvals
Drive the campus with Ralph -- identify which remaining parcels have best substation line-of-sight
Prepare a 1-page data center use case brief for Dudley showing MW value vs. industrial/office rents
brief.bpu_contact_relevant: Critical -- PSEG territory. BPU contact should confirm Whippany substation capacity before Brian pitches DC use to Dudley/Russo. Power confirmation is what differentiates Brian's pitch.
brief.competition_risk: high but mitigated by Brian relationships

briefs.name: Elmwood Park Power LLC
briefs.acquirability_notes: Requires further research on current ownership and status.

brief.opportunity_summary: Elmwood Park Power LLC is a 89MW retired natural gas combined cycle plant in NJ with existing transmission infrastructure (SADDLE BROOK 230kV substation 1.9 mi away).

acquirability.owner_motivation: Private/LLC ownership suggests direct negotiation possible.
acquirability.recommended_approach: Direct outreach to Elmwood Park Power LLC about site disposition or ground lease.
acquirability.notes: Requires further research on current ownership and status.

developer_value.comparable_transactions: NJ data center land trades at $300K-$1M/MW of supportable capacity in Tier 1 markets

next_steps: Verify current ownership via NJ County property records
Direct outreach to Elmwood Park Power LLC about site disposition or ground lease.
Confirm utility interconnection availability with PSEG commercial team
Request Phase I ESA or review existing environmental reports
Drive the site with Ralph -- assess physical condition, access, surrounding uses
brief.bpu_contact_relevant: Yes -- PSEG territory. Brian's BPU contacts can validate power availability.

briefs.name: Ahold eCommerce Sales Company Fulfillment Center
briefs.notes: Fulfillment and delivery services for Stop & Shop, shifting to store-based fulfillment

brief.opportunity_summary: Ahold eCommerce Sales Company Fulfillment Center is closing its distribution center facility in Not specified, displacing ~454 employees and an estimated 18MW of power demand. The utility infrastructure serving this site remains in place. Fulfillment and delivery services for Stop & Shop, shifting to store-based fulfillment

site_details.current_use: Closure in progress or recently completed. Site likely transitioning.
site_details.environmental_flags: Depends on prior industrial use. Phase I ESA recommended.

acquirability.owner_motivation: Company is leaving — they need to dispose of the site. Direct outreach to corporate RE.
acquirability.recommended_approach: Contact owner corporate real estate team about site disposition. WARN Act filing signals motivated timeline.

briefs.name: Henry Repeating Arms Manufacturing Plant
briefs.notes: Firearms manufacturer consolidating all operations to Wisconsin facilities. Company cited heavy manufacturing regulations in NJ as factor.

brief.opportunity_summary: Henry Repeating Arms Manufacturing Plant is closing its manufacturing facility in Bayonne, displacing ~142 employees and an estimated 10MW of power demand. The utility infrastructure serving this site remains in place. Firearms manufacturer consolidating all operations to Wisconsin facilities. Company cited heavy manufacturing regulations in NJ as factor.

briefs.name: Chambers Cogeneration LP
briefs.notes: 285MW retired coal plant in Salem County NJ that closed May 2022. Unlike sister plant Logan which was demolished, Chambers structures remain intact. Strong transmission infrastructure, adjacent to Chemours chemical complex, confirmed battery storage redevelopment plans indicate viable grid connection and regulatory approval pathway.
briefs.acquirability_notes: Joint ownership between Starwood Energy (60%) and Atlantic Power (40%) with committed battery storage redevelopment plans but no active demolition yet
briefs.current_owner: Starwood Energy Group Global LLC (60%), Atlantic Power Corporation (40%)
briefs.redevelopment_plans: Battery energy storage facility development as part of $1 billion joint venture with Energy Management Inc to store renewable energy
briefs.partnership_path: Approach Starwood Energy Group directly as they are leading the battery storage redevelopment, or potentially work with both Starwood and Atlantic Power given joint ownership structure

brief.opportunity_summary: Chambers Cogeneration LP is a 285MW retired coal plant in NJ with existing transmission infrastructure (CHAMBERS 230kV substation 0.08 mi away). 285MW retired coal plant in Salem County NJ that closed May 2022. Unlike sister plant Logan which was demolished, Chambers structures remain intact. Strong transmission infrastructure, adjacent to Chemours chemical complex, confirmed battery storage redevelopment plans indicate viable grid connection and regulatory approval pathway.

briefs.name: PSEG Essex Generating Station
briefs.notes: Plant closed in 2022 after transitioning from coal to gas peaking facility. Major environmental contamination contributor to Diamond Alkali Superfund site on Passaic River. Located in environmental justice community with persistent air quality issues. Switch house remains but main turbine hall was demolished. Site has documented history of kerosene leaks, oil spills, and regulatory violations dating to 1970s.
briefs.acquirability_notes: Site remains owned by PSEG with extensive environmental contamination history and ongoing Superfund liabilities. Major environmental justice concerns in Ironbound neighborhood. Property appears abandoned but still under utility control with active environmental monitoring obligations.
briefs.partnership_path: Direct negotiation with PSEG would be required, but significant environmental remediation costs and Superfund site proximity make acquisition challenging. Would need extensive environmental due diligence given plant's contribution to Passaic River contamination and ongoing EPA oversight.

brief.opportunity_summary: PSEG Essex Generating Station is a 595MW retired natural gas combustion turbine plant in NJ with existing transmission infrastructure (ESSEX 230kV substation 0.08 mi away). Plant closed in 2022 after transitioning from coal to gas peaking facility. Major environmental contamination contributor to Diamond Alkali Superfund site on Passaic River. Located in environmental justice community with persistent air quality issues. Switch house remains but main turbine hall was demolished. Site has documented history of kerosene leaks, oil spills, and regulatory violations dating to 1970s.

acquirability.owner_motivation: Private/LLC ownership suggests direct negotiation possible.
acquirability.recommended_approach: Direct outreach to PSEG Fossil LLC about site disposition or ground lease.
acquirability.notes: Site remains owned by PSEG with extensive environmental contamination history and ongoing Superfund liabilities. Major environmental justice concerns in Ironbound neighborhood. Property appears abandoned but still under utility control with active environmental monitoring obligations.
